rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Gorden N Ball, Inc'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANTS LENNAR CORPORATION, LENNAR HOMES OF CALIFORNIA INC, LENNAR-BVHP LLC, LENNAR ASSOCIATES MANAGEMENT LLC, AND, LENNAR COMMUNITIES, INC. Joinder To Gorden N Ball, Inc's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. See ruling on Defendant Gordon N. Ball Inc.'s Motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Request For Judicial Notice To Support Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT GORDON N BALL INC. Motion For Judgment on The Pleadings and Request for Judicial Notice to Support Judgment on The Pleadings. Motion denied. The alleged violations are ongoing. (See Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. vs. Kintetsu Enterprises of America. (2007) 150 Cal.App. 4th 953.) Although the law of the case doctrine does not apply because the Court of Appeal only considered the technical defect in Plaintiff's corporate status. However, the 60-day notice satisfied regulatory requirements and purposes. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Motion granted. The proposed amendment does not prejudice defendants and relates back to the original complaint. Tort claims concern the same general set of facts alleged in the original complaint that defendants were and continue to release asbestos. No conflict created by Plaintiff prosecuting both the Proposition 65 claim and the proposed Tort claim. Plaintiff requests injunctive relief for the UCL claim. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARDEN VANUPP VS. DAN MCCALL & ASSOCIATES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Of Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT DAN MCCALL & ASSOCIATES, INC. Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Is Granted with leave to amend. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAL SORENSON VS. CAROL LYNLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT LISA LANDEY Motion To Dismiss Action. Motion denied. The defendant was added by amendment to complaint on May 8, 2009 and served less than one month later. The action is deemed commenced as to the new defendant from the date of the filing of the amendment. (See Hennessey's Tavern, Inc. vs. American Air Filter Co. (1988) 204 Cal.App. 3d 1351, 1357, where new defendant was allegedly alter egos of defendant named in original complaint.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 21, Plaintiffs JT Ventures Partners Cochin Fund, L.P. JT Venture Partners Alleppey Fund, L.P. Windspeed Ventures, L.P., Freedom Hill Capital, Adams Cohen Ventures Capital LLC, and James Cunningham's (redacted) Motion for leave to file second amended complaint. rulings.Case Title: PAUL GUESS et al VS. DELAWARE NORTH COMPANIES PARKS AND RESORTS, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT DNC PARKS & RESORTS AT TENAYA, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied. Defendant's separate statement does not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350(d) separately identifing each cause of action. The notice does not comply with CRC 3.1350(b) specifing causes of action to be adjudicated. On the merits, motion denied for failure to shift burden of proof. Insufficient legally admissible evidence presented to shift the burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. ARROWOOD INDEMNITY COMPANY F/K/A ROYAL INDEMNITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens And, Alternatively, Based On Principles Of Comity And The Prevention Of Multiple And Vexatious Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 17, DEFENDANTS ARROWOOD INDEMNITY COMPANY F/K/A ROYAL INDEMNITY, AND, UNITED STATES FIDELITY AND GUARANTY COMPANY Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens and, Alternatively, Based On Principles Of Comity and The Prevention Of Multiple And Vexatious Litigation. Motion denied. The key issue in the coverage dispute is the Industrial Indemnity policy and its interplay with the Arrowood and USF&G policies. Most likely, California law will apply. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL K LIU et al VS. SALLY L LIU et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 18, DEFENDANT SALLY LIU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. Complaint alleges defendant's improper conduct is continuing. Facts alleged indicate that defendant may be estopped from raising any statute of limitation issue. (See Battuello vs. Battuello (1998) 64 Cal.App. 4th 842. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 6, 2009, line 19, PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of material fact raised regarding defendant's attempts to submit documentation regarding her unemployment benefits. (Defendant's declaration, page. 4.) =(302/CWW)
